talk_metadata.talk_title: Germline DAM2 R404W variant drives glioma susceptibility
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the overexpression here is limited to just the tumor cells, and these mice are also immunodeficient, so lacking the immunocompetent system that human patients have."
Speaker said: "I haven't looked at those, but it would be interesting to dive deeper."

key_data_gaps: No orthotopic immunocompetent tumor model demonstrated
Mechanistic link to Wnt signaling not shown

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of validated biomarkers linking DAM2 R404W to patient prognosis; CrisPRO could develop a splicing‑based biomarker panel to predict susceptibility.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we haven't really seen how this R414W variant actually plays into the role of the canonical role that it has. It has a role in Wnt signaling, and I don't really see any evidence of promoting Wnt signaling or any kind of gain-of-function phenotype from this."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's computational spine to integrate RNA‑seq and splicing data from DAM2 mutant tumors to derive predictive signatures.

unresolved_questions: Does the DAM2 R404W variant affect Wnt signaling in glioma?
What is the cell‑intrinsic versus extrinsic mechanism driving glial progenitor expansion?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Texas at San Antonio
talk_metadata.talk_title: Age‑dependent mammary tumor genesis driven by mutant PIK3CA
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "I don't think it can affect our model because the estrogen alpha receptor is expressed in the tumor, so tumor cells is actually producing the ER alpha."
Speaker said: "We see a higher tumor incidence and also early onset compared to the young mice."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of hormonal (estrogen) modulation in aged mice may mask true tumor growth dynamics

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: PIK3CA H1047R oncogenic signaling in mammary epithelium
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Mice not stratified by hormonal status (e.g., ovariectomy) despite age‑related estrogen changes
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Serum estrogen levels or ERα status in injected ducts
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small cohort per age group limits power to detect age‑specific effects

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted difficulty targeting the bladder‑relevant E545K hotspot due to PAM constraints, indicating platform limitation.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single hotspot mutation may limit translational relevance to human breast cancer heterogeneity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that aged mice have higher tumor incidence yet slower tumor growth, then concluded that targeting age‑associated epithelial changes may offer prevention opportunities – contradictory because high incidence suggests a strong oncogenic drive despite slower growth.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic partners for PIK3CA‑mutant tumors in aged microenvironment, e.g., endocrine modulators.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Removing... ovariectomy... I don't think it can affect our model because the estrogen alpha receptor is expressed in the tumor."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model pathway cross‑talk between PI3K and estrogen signaling to prioritize combination candidates.

crispro_opportunity.description: Lack of biomarkers to predict which aged mice will develop tumors; CrisPRO could generate a transcriptomic risk score.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The aged mice shows higher tumor incidence and also early onset compared to the young mice."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply CrisPRO's machine‑learning spine to integrate basal/luminal cell ratios and PI3K activity for risk stratification.

unresolved_questions: Would ovariectomy alter tumor initiation in the aged model?
How does multiple pregnancy affect the basal/luminal cell dynamics and tumor susceptibility?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Modeling bladder cancer PIK3 pathway alterations with CRISPR organoids and CAF co‑engraftment
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We saw that the tumors do shrink in both the hotspot organoid line as well as the baseline organoid that doesn't have the hotspot."
Speaker said: "The GM‑CSF knockout did not reliably shrink tumor formation."

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Alpelisib in PIK3CA‑mutant bladder organoid xenografts
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: CAF‑mediated immunosuppression masks genotype‑specific drug sensitivity
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "the GM‑CSF knockout did not reliably shrink tumor formation."
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker noted shrinkage in both hotspot and wild‑type organoids, implying a non‑specific effect.

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: PIK3CA hotspot activation in bladder cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Engraftment only in immunocompromised mice or with exogenous CAFs, not reflecting native tumor‑immune context
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Endogenous CAF markers to stratify tumors before implantation
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial ctDNA or immune profiling during treatment
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small n per genotype limits detection of genotype‑specific effects

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Reliance on Cre‑inducible Cas9 mouse line and luciferase labeling may be circumvented by alternative labeling strategies.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Need for CAF co‑injection reduces scalability for high‑throughput screening

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed genotype‑specific sensitization to alpelisib in vitro, then presented in vivo data where both hotspot and wild‑type tumors responded similarly, contradicting the earlier claim of genotype‑specific efficacy.

key_data_gaps: Lack of orthotopic bladder tumor model
No immune profiling (e.g., phospho‑STAT3) to link cytokine crosstalk to response

crispro_opportunity.description: Target IL‑6/GM‑CSF axis identified in CAF‑organoid crosstalk to overcome immunosuppression and improve alpelisib efficacy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We found that IL‑6 was coming from the CAFs, and the GM‑CSF was coming from the organoids."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate cytokine network perturbations to prioritize combination inhibitors (e.g., JAK inhibitors) with alpelisib.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a fully immunocompetent orthotopic bladder organoid model eliminating need for exogenous CAFs.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "They don't grow orthotopically. And then we started putting them in flank engraftment and they start to regress."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage CrisPRO's organoid‑to‑patient translation framework to design microenvironment‑aware preclinical pipelines.

cited_competitors.name: Alpelisib (Piqray)
cited_competitors.context: Clinically approved PI3K inhibitor tested on organoids

cited_competitors.name: Cisplatin
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care chemotherapy used as comparator

unresolved_questions: Does phospho‑STAT3 activation occur in CAF‑co‑engrafted tumors?
Can IL‑6 blockade synergize with alpelisib in vivo?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Patient‑derived organoids and multi‑omic profiling to predict immunotherapy response in NSCLC
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "The patient‑derived organoids established from the patient classified as responder ... were actually presenting an enrichment in the activation of the inflammatory associated pathway."
Speaker said: "The penetration of CD45 positive cells was much higher in the patient‑derived organoids classified as not responder."

key_data_gaps: No functional validation that organoid immune infiltration predicts patient outcome
Missing comparison to standard PD‑L1 biomarker

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑derived composite biomarker integrating organoid cytokine response and spatial transcriptomics to outperform PD‑L1.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "This dichotomy, not responder, responder, do not significantly correlate with any clinical associated info, including PD‑L1 status or the mutation."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse multi‑omic layers (spatial, bulk RNA, cytokine) into a predictive score.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify agents that convert the high CD45‑infiltrated, exhausted phenotype in non‑responders into a productive anti‑tumor response.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The CD8 infiltrated the not responder organoids were actually characterized by a predominantly exhaustion profile."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to simulate checkpoint blockade plus epigenetic modulators to reverse exhaustion signatures.

cited_competitors.name: PD‑L1 testing
cited_competitors.context: Standard biomarker that showed no correlation in this cohort

unresolved_questions: What drives the higher CD45 infiltration in non‑responders?
Can the Notch pathway differences observed in spatial data explain response heterogeneity?

talk_metadata.talk_title: 3D biosensing nanobioreactor to visualize APOBEC3‑driven splicing dynamics in aged bone marrow
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We see a shift from red to green between the PCH to A3C samples."
Speaker said: "We do think that it happens directly through RNA editing."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted the system is patented but not yet commercialized, indicating potential for external licensing.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Current system appears low‑throughput and requires specialized equipment.

key_data_gaps: Direct demonstration that APOBEC3C edits splice sites is missing
In vivo relevance of observed exon‑skipping not shown

crispro_opportunity.description: Model APOBEC3C‑mediated splicing alterations to predict emergence of pre‑cancer clones and test splicing modulators.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We see an increase in GFP expression in the APOBEC3C transduced aged bone marrow cells, as well as a decrease in the shift of RFP to GFP expression throughout these cells."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate APOBEC3C‑induced editing on splice donor/acceptor motifs to prioritize splicing‑targeted therapeutics.

unresolved_questions: Does nuclear versus cytoplasmic localization of APOBEC3C affect splicing outcomes?
Can the nanobioreactor be adapted for high‑throughput drug screening?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Ex vivo micro‑tumor platform predicts ADC and combination responses in ovarian cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We see a very nice correlation of the ADC MIRF to paclitaxel, while this is less the case for carboplatin and topotecan."
Speaker said: "We don't passage it. So the whole assay really from sample collection to having the results is about 10 days."

trial_dilution_risk.trial_name_or_nct: Retrospective ex vivo ADC response correlation study
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Samples collected up to 4 months post‑chemotherapy show reduced culture success, potentially biasing toward treatment‑naïve tumors
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Limited number of patients (n=25) for ADC response analysis

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Proprietary hydrogel matrix for micro‑tumor culture
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker described it as a proprietary mix; details not disclosed, may be reverse‑engineered.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires fresh patient tissue; scalability limited by biopsy availability.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Platform currently validated only in ovarian cancer; broader tumor types need further data.

key_data_gaps: Mechanistic link between antigen heterogeneity and ADC efficacy not fully explored

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate CrisPRO predictive modeling to select patients with high FRα heterogeneity for ADC trials, improving enrichment.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We observed quite some intra‑patient heterogeneity in this, as well as inter‑patient heterogeneity in a sense that, for example, for patient one, we see a rather broad expression of the ADC target antigen at a moderate expression level."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to simulate antigen distribution scenarios and forecast response rates for trial stratification.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify synergistic partners (e.g., PARP inhibitors) for FRα‑ADC based on ex vivo synergy data.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We see a nice synergistic effect... for some patients, but not in others."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model DNA repair deficiency interactions with ADC payloads to prioritize combos.

cited_competitors.name: Mirvetuximab soravtansine
cited_competitors.context: FRα‑targeting ADC evaluated in the platform

cited_competitors.name: Olaparib
cited_competitors.context: PARP inhibitor combined with ADC in ex vivo tests

unresolved_questions: How does prior chemotherapy timing affect ex vivo culture success?
Can the platform predict long‑term clinical outcomes beyond progression‑free survival?

talk_metadata.talk_title: eLive platform: ex vivo cytokine profiling of fresh tumor fragments predicts ICI response
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We have 93% of concordance of what we have done ex vivo and what the patient had received in the clinic and the observed clinical responses."

Speaker said: "Data science have done a lot of analysis on trying to predict the patient responder in the clinic with only interferon gamma or interferon gamma and CXCL10 does not give us this 93% correlation."

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Immune checkpoint inhibitor efficacy in solid tumors
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Single core needle biopsy per patient may miss intratumoral heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Spatial mapping of immune infiltrates beyond cytokine panel
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No longitudinal cytokine sampling during treatment
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Cohort size (28 patients) limits robustness of 93% concordance claim

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: eLive proprietary cutting and gel‑encapsulation method
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Method described in detail during talk; may be replicable without licensing.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires fresh biopsy processing within narrow time window.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Limited to settings where biopsy material is available; not applicable to radiologic‑only assessments.

key_data_gaps: Correlation of subcluster phenotypes with overall survival

crispro_opportunity.description: Expand CrisPRO to integrate the nine‑cytokine eLive index with spatial transcriptomics for a more robust predictive model.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The eLive index, which is our prediction algorithm that is based in the nine most upregulated cytokines."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can fuse cytokine signatures with spatial immune cell maps to improve responder prediction beyond 93%.

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify agents that convert subcluster four (polyfunctional with VEGF) into a more T‑cell inflamed phenotype.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Subcluster four is the most robust out of all subclusters with a very complex tumor microenvironment... we hypothesize not only doing immune checkpoint inhibitors and monotherapy, also combining with VEGF inhibitors."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate VEGF blockade impact on cytokine network using CrisPRO to prioritize combos.

unresolved_questions: Do the four cytokine subclusters predict duration of response or overall survival?
Can interferon‑gamma alone ever serve as a sufficient predictor in any subset?
